The study examines implications for volcano distribution, plate coupling, and the structural characteristics of the normal-to-flat slab transition, with the Coastal Cordillera acting as a subduction wedge.
papers.relevance_notes: Derives high-resolution 3D seismic tomography from passive seismic network data along the transition zone of north-central Chile (22°–31°S); integrates with marine seismic refraction velocity models from multiple prior cruise datasets
papers.geographic_focus: North Chile / Central Chile (22°–31°S, normal-to-flat slab transition)

papers.title: Structural Characterization of the Taltal Segment in Northern Chile Between 22°S and 26°S Using Local Earthquake Tomography
papers.abstract_snippet: A temporary deployment of 84 short-period seismometers in northern Chile is used to derive regional 3D seismic velocity models for the Taltal segment (22°S–26°S). An earthquake catalog of 23,985 hypocenters was created using automatic onset estimation. 3D Vp, Vs and Vp/Vs models from local earthquake tomography reveal active structures in the upper crust, slab dip changes, and fracturing within the oceanic crust. Plots of hypocenters reveal a double seismic zone and fluid pathways in the subducting slab.
papers.relevance_notes: Uses local earthquake tomography from a dense seismic network including offshore OBH instruments in the Taltal segment of northern Chile (22°–26°S); contributes to structural characterization of the north Chilean marine forearc

papers.title: Seismo-Turbidites in Aysén Fjord (Southern Chile) Reveal a Complex Pattern of Rupture Modes Along the 1960 Megathrust Earthquake Segment
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
papers.abstract_snippet: Grain size analysis and end-member modeling of a long sediment core from Aysén Fjord (southern Chile, ~45.5°S) identifies over 25 seismo-turbidites in the last 9,000 years. The paleoseismic record documents a recurrence rate of 321 ± 116 years for the Valdivia segment in the last two millennia, and infers large temporal variability in rupture modes, with full-segment ruptures alternating with partial and cascading ruptures. The study uses subbottom CHIRP profiles and seismic reflection data to interpret fjord sedimentary structures.
papers.relevance_notes: Uses CHIRP subbottom profiler and seismic reflection data from Aysén Fjord (~45.5°S) combined with sediment cores to document paleoseismic record; marine seismic data in southern Chilean fjord setting provides structural context for turbidite interpretation

papers.title: Forearc structures and tectonics in the southern Peru—northern Chile Continental Margin
papers.abstract_snippet: SeaMARC II side-scan images, bathymetry, and single-channel seismic reflection data along the southern Peru—northern Chile forearc between 16° and 23°S reveal a complex region of morpho-structural, submarine drainage and depression patterns. Two major fault systems in the subducting plate comprise NW-SE primary trench-parallel normal faults and NE-SW secondary normal faults formed by plate bending. Overriding continental plate structures include slope basins and troughs distributed along the margin. The forearc morphology reflects the dominance of subduction erosion along this segment.
papers.relevance_notes: Reports SeaMARC II side-scan imagery, bathymetry and single-channel seismic reflection data from the southern Peru–northern Chile forearc (16°–23°S); directly maps forearc structures from marine seismic and acoustic data
papers.geographic_focus: North Chile (16°–23°S, southern Peru–northern Chile forearc)

papers.title: Pleistocene giant slope failures offshore Arauco Peninsula, Southern Chile
papers.abstract_snippet: Reflection seismic and swath bathymetric data from offshore Arauco Peninsula reveal three giant submarine slope failures. These slope failures rank among the largest submarine slides known from active continental margins. The locations of the slope failures coincide with a major earthquake segment boundary: the region to the south ruptured during the 1960 Mw 9.5 Great Chile Earthquake, while the region to the north ruptured during the 2010 Mw 8.8 Maule Earthquake. The data suggest that tectonic and structural controls at the segment boundary drove gravitational instability of the forearc.
papers.relevance_notes: Uses marine seismic reflection profiles and swath bathymetry to image three giant submarine slope failures offshore Arauco Peninsula (~38°S); directly reports marine seismic data used to characterize the slide morphology and extent

papers.title: Sedimentation in the Chile Trench: Depositional morphologies, lithofacies, and stratigraphy
papers.abstract_snippet: The depositional bodies of the Chile Trench (trench fans, axial channel, sheeted basins, ponded basins, and axial sediment lobes) control the spatial distribution of modern lithofacies. Five lithofacies are defined from Q-mode factor analysis of 27 cores. Sheeted basins south of 41°S are fed by closely-spaced submarine gullies, while trench fans between 33°–41°S form at the mouths of major submarine canyon systems. The stratigraphy shows cyclicity linked to Milankovitch cycles in glacial-interglacial sediment flux.
papers.relevance_notes: Uses multichannel seismic reflection profiles to map depositional morphologies in the Chile Trench (33°–47°S); seismic stratigraphy defines trench fill architecture and provides the structural framework for core site selection

papers.title: Crustal structure and deep seismogenic zone of the Nazca plate offshore southern Chile from wide-angle seismic data: Implications for fluid-related processes and seismic coupling
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
papers.abstract_snippet: Wide-angle seismic data from two profiles across the Nazca plate subducting at the south-central Chile trench are used to derive a 2D Vp and Poisson's ratio model of the incoming oceanic plate. P-wave velocities in the outer rise/trench zone are significantly reduced compared to old Pacific plate, indicating 2–6% serpentinization of the uppermost oceanic mantle. Serpentinization of the incoming plate at the outer rise controls the amount of water input to the subduction zone and affects the seismogenic coupling of the plate interface.
papers.relevance_notes: Reports wide-angle seismic refraction data from two marine profiles across the incoming Nazca plate at the south-central Chile trench; derives Vp and Poisson's ratio models from OBS/OBH data to quantify serpentinization

papers.title: Sediment subduction and accretion at the Chilean convergent margin between 35° and 40°S
papers.journal: Ph.D. thesis, Christian-Albrechts-Universität Kiel / GEOMAR Report
papers.abstract_snippet: Seismic reflection profiles, swath bathymetric data, and heat flow measurements were combined to characterize sediment subduction and accretion processes along the Chilean convergent margin between 35° and 40°S. The data reveal the geometry of the accretionary prism and quantify mass fluxes of incoming and accreted sediments. The study provides the first systematic compilation of marine seismic lines from this sector of the south-central Chile margin.
papers.relevance_notes: Presents marine seismic reflection profiles and heat flow data from south-central Chile (35°–40°S); foundational GEOMAR report on sediment accretion/subduction using named survey lines from this sector

papers.title: Seismic imaging of the underplated lower continental crust of south-central Chile
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
papers.abstract_snippet: Analysis of 2017 CEVICHE (MGL1701) seismic data using full waveform inversion provides velocity models for the lower continental crust of south-central Chile, revealing underplated material beneath the forearc. High-velocity anomalies at 15–30 km depth are interpreted as basal accretion of crustal slices from the subducting Nazca plate. The spatial distribution of underplated material correlates with the rupture limits of major megathrust earthquakes, suggesting that underplated material controls seismogenic segmentation.
papers.relevance_notes: Uses full waveform inversion of 2017 MGL1701 CEVICHE marine seismic data from south-central Chile to image underplated lower continental crust; extends basal accretion findings from Bangs et al. 2020 using the same survey
papers.geographic_focus: Central Chile / South Chile (34°–42°S, CEVICHE survey area)

papers.title: Upper and lower plate structure at the convergent margin north of Antofagasta, Chile: evidence from wide-angle P- and S-wave modelling
papers.journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
papers.abstract_snippet: Wide-angle P- and S-wave data from an amphibious OBH/OBS profile at the convergent margin north of Antofagasta (~21.5°S) are modelled to derive the seismic velocity structure of both the upper and lower plate. The margin is characterised by a thin sediment veneer and lacks an accretionary prism. Upper plate velocities of 4–6.8 km/s indicate a fractured continental basement. The subducted oceanic crust is imaged as a layer of 6–7 km/s overlying a mantle reflector.
papers.relevance_notes: Reports wide-angle seismic refraction OBH/OBS data from an amphibious profile at north Chile (~21.5°S, Iquique region); directly characterizes upper and lower plate velocity structure from marine seismic data
papers.geographic_focus: North Chile (~21.5°S, north of Antofagasta / Iquique region)

papers.title: Subduction Erosion — the 'Normal' Mode of Fore-Arc Material Transfer along the Chilean Margin?
papers.journal: The Andes: Frontiers in Earth Sciences (Springer)
papers.abstract_snippet: Subduction erosion along the Chilean margin is examined using multichannel seismic reflection data to quantify the mass flux of eroded material and the long-term evolution of the fore-arc. The study addresses whether subduction erosion is the 'normal' or dominant mode of material transfer, using mass-balance calculations from seismic profiles to constrain rates of crustal thinning and slope retreat along the north and central Chilean margin.
papers.relevance_notes: Reviews subduction erosion mass flux using multichannel seismic reflection profiles from the Chilean margin; synthesizes seismic data from CINCA and other cruises to constrain material transfer at the north and central Chilean fore-arc
papers.geographic_focus: North Chile / Central Chile (subduction erosion synthesis)

papers.title: Seismic structure of the incoming oceanic Nazca Plate, outer rise, and trench at the Chile Triple Junction area
papers.abstract_snippet: Wide-angle seismic refraction data from an OBS profile across the incoming Nazca plate at the Chile Triple Junction (~45.5°S) document the seismic velocity structure of the oceanic crust and uppermost mantle. The Antarctic plate segment of the incoming plate shows significantly higher mantle velocities than the Pacific segment, suggesting less serpentinization due to younger and hotter plate age. P-wave velocities in the trench axis indicate a 2–4 km thick sediment column with typical trench-fill seismic characteristics.
papers.relevance_notes: Reports marine seismic refraction OBS data from a profile across the incoming Nazca and Antarctic plates at the Chile Triple Junction (~45.5°S); directly images oceanic crust and mantle velocity structure near the triple junction
papers.geographic_focus: South Chile (~45.5°S, Chile Triple Junction incoming plates)
